classification_routing.justification: processing_meta.json records no route/inferred_type (it is byte-identical to summary.json), so the exact route label is not recoverable from logs; however the supporting signals are fully consistent with expectations: summary.language 'en' matches the manifest, the deck is clearly a slide_pdf, and the output mixes clean per-slide text with vision-derived [Diagram:...] descriptions, which is what a hybrid text+vision route would produce.
evidence: [Diagram: This illustrates a binary filtering task

text_coverage.justification: All substantive text from all 9 source slides is present: every definition/citation, all T/P/E triples for the four example problems, both key-elements slides, both issues slides, and the learning-types slide; the six web-categorization labels and all diagram candidate words also match the source. No source section or bullet is omitted.

extraction_fidelity.justification: Character/word extraction is clean English with no garbling or mojibake; citations ('[Simon, 1983]', '[Alpaydin, 2004]') and label words (we, do, in, the, right, way; Go straight, Move left, ...) match the source exactly, and the source ligature glitch 'classication' is silently normalized to 'classification'.

structure_preservation.justification: Headings, nested bullet hierarchies, and reading order are faithfully preserved and match the source: the two-column slide 4 is linearized left-to-right (Handwriting then Robot, matching source layout), and 'Planning' is correctly nested under 'Reinforcement learning' exactly as indented on source page 9.

faithfulness_no_hallucination.justification: The academic slide content is faithful and the six web-category names are genuinely in the source, but the diagram synthesis injects invented material: each figure is given an editorial 'It matters because...' rationale absent from the source, and the filtering diagram invents leaf labels 'Interested'/'Uninterested' where the source figure actually branches to a photo of a person at a computer and a document/printer icon.
evidence: It matters because it reduces information overload by separating relevant content from irrelevant content.
| Interested|  | Uninterested | (source figure branches to a photo of a woman at a computer and a document/printer icon, not these text labels)

formula_table_code.justification: The only genuine math (the target-function mappings) is rendered correctly and legibly in LaTeX, and T/P/E are wrapped as inline math; there are no real tables or code, and the ASCII diagrams' factual labels (six categories, steering commands, candidate words) match the source.

classification.actual_type: slide_pdf (inferred from output; not recorded in processing_meta)
classification.actual_route: unrecoverable from logs (no route field); text+vision output consistent with hybrid

hallucinations: It matters because it reduces information overload by separating relevant content from irrelevant content.
It matters because it enables organizing large collections of web content for search, browsing, and recommendation.
It matters because the system learns from labeled examples to convert visual text into usable digital information.
It matters because the learned policy can imitate human driving behavior and support autonomous navigation.
Filtering-diagram leaf labels 'Interested' / 'Uninterested' (source figure branches to a photo of a woman at a computer and a document/printer icon, not text labels)
normalization_note: Markdown reads coherently end-to-end with no dropped or corrupted spans relative to the 9-slide source; all headings and nested bullets are intact, math is valid LaTeX, and the source ligature glitch 'classication' is normalized to 'classification'. The only integrity concerns are additive: invented per-diagram 'It matters because...' commentary and two fabricated leaf labels in the filtering figure.
overall_comment: Strong, near-complete digitization of the English slide deck: 100% text coverage across all 9 slides, clean OCR, correctly rendered LaTeX formulas, and structure (headings, nested lists, slide-4 left-to-right order, 'Planning' nested under Reinforcement) that faithfully matches the source. The six web-category labels are genuinely present in the source and are NOT hallucinations. The main weakness is hallucination confined to the diagram descriptions - four invented 'It matters because...' rationales plus fabricated 'Interested'/'Uninterested' leaf labels. Routing passes: route was not persisted in processing_meta, but language ('en') and slide_pdf/hybrid signals (clean text + vision-derived diagram descriptions) are consistent with expectations.
